sections.paragraph: & מִגְדֹּל [Migdol], pr. name of a town of Lower Egypt, Jer 44:1 46:14 situated in the most northern part of the boundaries of Egypt, Eze 29:10 30:6 we are not to regard as different from this, Exo 14:2 Num 33:7 (see Thes. p. 268). This name is written in Egyptian ⲙⲉϣⲧⲱⲗ (abundance of hills), which as a foreign name the Hebrews appear to have changed into מִגְדֹּל (tower); see Champollion, l’Egypte sous les Pharaons, ii. page 79.
